summary: A crisp soundboard upgrade captures a joyous 1985 set with standout Alabama, Jack Straw and a superb She Belongs To Me despite a brief splice.
review: The upgraded SBD transfer is clear and well‑mastered, though a five‑second splice in “She Belongs To Me” remains. Musically the show shines – Set 1 opens with a ripping ‘Alabama’, delivers tight renditions of Jack Straw, Dire Wolf/El Paso/She Belongs To Me and Brother Esau/Friend of the Devil, and the second set rolls through Crazy Fingers, The Wheel, Black Peter, Around and Lovelight with the band clearly having fun. Overall the performance is highly enjoyable and the recording quality solid.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers gave 4‑star scores and praised the musical flow while noting a minor splice; recording is good, show is strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Improved transfer and mastering give crisp clarity; a ~5‑second splice in “She Belongs To Me” at ~4:10 remains, but it is patchable from other sources.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set 1 builds strong energy with tight song pairings; Set 2 maintains momentum through jams and seamless transitions, showcasing the band’s joy.

band_member_comments.Drums: Drums are highlighted as special and energetic by a reviewer who recalls them as a key part of the experience.
